category_title: Children with ASD exhibiting disruptive sensory-seeking and out-of-seat behaviors
student_description: Matthew is an 8-year-old boy with ASD who exhibits disruptive sensory-seeking behaviors, specifically touching or pulling peers' hair (especially if they wear sparkly grips), and frequent out-of-seat behavior. Because his behaviors cause physical pain and distress to peers, standard peer-ignoring strategies fail. He uniquely requires highly structured visual feedback systems, tangible Differential Reinforcement of Other behavior (DRO), controlled desensitization to his sensory triggers, and carefully calibrated academic tasks.

criteria.rationale: Matthew needs safe, controlled exposure to his specific sensory triggers (sparkly hair grips) to practice omitting the hair-touching behavior without harming peers.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes staff intentionally wearing or introducing the trigger items (sparkly grips) in a controlled setting while reinforcing Matthew for not touching them.

criteria.rationale: Ensuring academic targets are at the correct difficulty level prevents task-avoidance from contributing to his out-of-seat behavior.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates regular reviews of Matthew's curriculum and task difficulty to ensure he can successfully engage and earn reinforcement.

criteria.rationale: To maintain 100% in-seat behavior over weeks, Matthew requires daily visual successes to build toward a highly motivating, novel weekly reward.
criteria.check: The long-term plan links daily behavioral successes (e.g., earning daily stickers or tokens) to a larger, delayed tangible reward (e.g., a weekly surprise toy).
